LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS'(PCC) MOTION TO STAY ACTION PENDING APPEAL OF RULING ON DEMURRER. THE MOTION FOR STAY THE ENTIRE ACTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT'S ORDER OF AUGUST 2, 2013, IS APPEALABLE PER CC 1714.10(D) AND EVANS V. PILLSBURY, MADISON & SUTRO (1998) 65 CAL.APP.4TH 599, 602-604. THE AUTOMATIC STAY PROVISION OF CCP 916 APPLIES. WHILE CC 1714.10(D) STATES THAT THE PROCEDURE IS A "SPECIAL PROCEEDING OF A CIVIL NATURE," IT ALSO STATES THAT AN ORDER RESOLVING THE MATTER "SHALL BE APPEALABLE AS A FINAL JUDGMENT IN A CIVIL ACTION." ID. THIS LAST PORTION OF SUBSECTION (D) SUGGESTS THAT THE APPEAL IS TANTAMOUNT TO AN APPEAL IN A CIVIL ACTION AND THUS THAT CCP 916 APPLIES. A STAY OF THE ENTIRE ACTION IS ALSO REASONABLE TO INSURE THAT PROCEEDINGS IN THIS COURT DO NOT CREATE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST BETWEEN ATTORNEY GAREN AND HIS CLIENT DEFENDANT PCC, WHICH MAY AFFECT THE APPEAL AND RENDER THE APPEAL INEFFECTIVE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROBERT LUJAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN FAVOR OF LUJAN ON THE COMPLAINT - THE COURT GRANTS MR. LUJAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE COMPLAINT. THIS IS AN ACTION TO RECOVER A DEBT INCURRED UNDER THE CREDIT AGREEMENT. THE AGREEMENT CONTAINED A DELAWARE CHOICE OF LAW PROVISION, WHICH MEANS THAT THE THREE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PROVIDED IN 10 DELAWARE CODE SECTION 8106 APPLIED. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT THE COMMON COUNTS REQUIRE AN APPLICATION OF CALIFORNIA'S FOUR YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS UNPERSUASIVE. MONIES DUE UNDER AN EXPRESS WRITTEN CONTRACT, SUCH AS THE CARDMEMBER AGREEMENT AT ISSUE HERE, "CANNOT, IN THE ABSENCE OF A CONTRARY AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES, BE TREATED AS ITEMS UNDER AN OPEN BOOK ACCOUNT SO AS TO ALLOW THE UNPAID CREDITOR TO EVADE OR EXTEND THE STATUTORY LIMITATIONS PERIOD." (TSEMETZIN V. COAST FEDERAL SAVINGS & LOAN ASSOC. (1997) 57 CAL.APP.4TH 1334, 1343.) CALIFORNIA LAW ESTABLISHES THAT A DEBT WHICH IS BASED UPON THE BREACH OF THE TERMS OF AN EXPRESS CONTRACT CANNOT BE THE BASIS OF AN ACCOUNT STATED. (MOORE V. BARTHOLOMAE CORP. (1945) 69 CAL.APP.2D 474, 477-478.) THE DEBT HERE IS BASED ON DEFENDANT LUJAN'S ALLEGED BREACH OF THE CARDMEMBER AGREEMENT. THE CASES PLAINTIFF RELIES UPON IN SUPPORT OF ITS POSITION AND TO OPPOSE DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ARE NOT BINDING ON THIS COURT. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FILED THE INSTANT OUTSIDE OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PROVIDED UNDER 10 DELAWARE CODE SECTION 8106, DEFENDANT LUJAN IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE COMPLAINT. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROBERT LUJAN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS ON APPEAL IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $105,459. THE APPEAL YIELDED A PUBLISHED DECISION IN AN IMPORTANT FIELD OF LAW. I REVIEWED COUNSELS' TIME ENTRIES AND HOURLY RATES AND FIND THEM REASONABLE BASED ON MY 32 YEARS' EXPERIENCE AS A LITIGATOR AND THEN JUDGE IN THIS LEGAL MARKET. (SEE SERRANO V. PRIEST (1977) 20 CAL.3D 25, 49.) A MULTIPLIER OF 1.5 TO THE LODESTAR AMOUNT WAS APPLIED IN THE TRIAL COURT PROCEEDINGS AND AFFIRMED BY THE COURT OF APPEAL; I FIND THE SAME MULTIPLIER APPROPRIATE ON APPEAL. THE AWARD IS CALCULATED THUS: $65,360 ATTORNEY FEES X 1.5 ($98,040) + $7,200 IN FEES FOR THIS MOTION + $219 IN COSTS = $105,459. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Robert Lujan's motion for attorney fees and costs on appeal is GRANTED in the amount of $105,459. The appeal yielded a published decision in an important field of law. I reviewed counsels' time entries and hourly rates and find them reasonable based on my 32 years' experience as a litigator and then judge in this legal market. (See Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49.) A multiplier of 1.5 to the lodestar amount was applied in the trial court proceedings and affirmed by the Court of Appeal; I find the same multiplier appropriate on appeal. The award is calculated thus: $65,360 attorney fees x 1.5 ($98,040) + $7,200 in fees for this motion + $219 in costs = $105,459. Order signed in open court. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment By Robert M. Lujan, Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication In Favor Of Lujan On The Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Clark Garen of KAKZ Television telephone number 760-323-4901 for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Professional Collection Consultants
Raeon Roulston of Consumer Law Center, Inc. telephone number 408-294-6100 for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Robert Lujan.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS COMPLAINANT ROBERT LUJAN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication In Favor Of Lujan On The Cross-Complaint - Mr. Lujan's motion for summary judgment on the cross-complaint is denied in part and granted in part. To prevail on the FDCPA and the RFDCPA claims, Lujan must establish that the cross-defendants were debt collectors. (Wolkow v. Scottsdale Collection Serv., LLC (2010) LEXIS 103786 at 8-9.) Here, Mr. Lujan's motion for summary judgment on the cross-complaint as to cross-defendant Wireless is denied because the evidence provided merely establishes that Wireless was an intermediate assignee/assignor of the subject account and when a creditor sells an account to another entity, it does not directly or indirectly attempt to collect the debt. (Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts on Lujan's Cross-complaint # 6; Neff v. Capital Acquisitions & Mgmt. Co. (7th Cir. 2003) 352 F.3d 1118, 1121.) Mr. Lujan's motion for summary judgment on the cross-complaint as to cross-defendant Clark Garen is also denied. Cross-defendants allege that Defendant Garen is not in the business of collecting debt. In response, Lujan states that Garen is a salaried employee of PCC, lead counsel in the action, and a managing member of Wireless. (Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts on Lujan's Cross-complaint # 7.) Lujan does not cite to evidence that Garen purchased the debt in his own name and a violation of Business and Professions Code section 6129 does not constitute a violation of FDCPA or RFDCPA. Mr. Lujan's motion for summary judgment on the cross-complaint as to cross-defendant Todd Shields is also denied because the evidence presented only establishes that he is the president of PCC and the he signed the Indemnity and Hold Harmless Agreement. (Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts on Lujan's Cross-complaint # 3-4.) Lujan has not presented evidence that Shields was involved with the collection of debt on Lujan's account. Mr. Lujan is entitled to summary judgment on the cross-complaint as to cross-defendant PCC. PCC is a debt collector. (Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts on Lujan's Cross-complaint # 1.) By filing the instant lawsuit and attempting to collect on the debt outside of the statute of limitations, PCC violated the FDCPA. (15 U.S.C. 1692e(2)(A). PCC is also liable under California Civil Code section 1788.17. When a debt collector violates the FDCPA, the debt collector is also in violation of the RFDCPA. (Hoesseinzadeh v. M.R.S. (2005) 387 F.Supp.2d 1104, 11118.) Because there is not a triable issue of material fact regarding the fact that PCC filed the instant lawsuit outside of the statute of limitations, Mr. Lujan is entitled to summary judgment on the cross-complaint as to cross-defendant PCC. Although Luajn is entitled to attorney's fees under 15 U.S.C. section 1692k(a)(3) and California Civil Code section 1788.30(c), Mr. Lujan has not provided the court with evidence for the court to determine the appropriate fees. Counsel for the prevailing party is to provide a form of order for the Court's consideration.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication In Favor Of Lujan On The Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Clark Garen of KAKZ Television telephone number 760-323-4901 for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Professional Collection Consultants
Raeon Roulston of Consumer Law Center, Inc. telephone number 408-294-6100 for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Robert Lujan.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT ROBERT LUJAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication In Favor Of Lujan On The Complaint - The court grants Mr. Lujan's motion for summary judgment on the complaint. This is an action to recover a debt incurred under the credit agreement. The agreement contained a Delaware choice of law provision, which means that the three year statute of limitations provided in 10 Delaware Code section 8106 applied. Plaintiff's argument that the common counts require an application of California's four year statute of limitations is unpersuasive. Monies due under an express written contract, such as the cardmember agreement at issue here, "cannot, in the absence of a contrary agreement between the parties, be treated as items under an open book account so as to allow the unpaid creditor to evade or extend the statutory limitations period." (Tsemetzin v. Coast Federal Savings & Loan Assoc. (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 1334, 1343.) California law establishes that a debt which is based upon the breach of the terms of an express contract cannot be the basis of an account stated. (Moore v. Bartholomae Corp. (1945) 69 Cal.App.2d 474, 477-478.) The debt here is based on Defendant Lujan's alleged breach of the cardmember agreement. The cases Plaintiff relies upon in support of its position and to oppose Defendant's motion for summary judgment are not binding on this court. Because Plaintiff filed the instant outside of the statute of limitations provided under 10 Delaware Code section 8106, Defendant Lujan is entitled to summary judgment on the complaint. Counsel for the prevailing party is to provide a form of order for the Court's consideration.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Ntc Of Mtn To Stay Action Pending Appeal Of Ruling On Demurrer
Off Calendar Sep-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Clark Garen, Esq. of the Law Offices of Clark Garen, Tel. 310.391.0800, for plaintiff and moving party, by Court Call;
Kimberly Alexander Kane, Esq. of Morgan Lewis, Tel. 415.442.1292, for defendant Chase Bank and opposing party;
Nathaniel Bigger, Esq. of the Law Offices of Nathaniel Bigger, Tel. 415.295.4735, for defendant Lujan.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS'(PCC) Motion To Stay Action Pending Appeal Of Ruling On Demurrer. The motion for stay the entire action is granted. The Court's order of August 2, 2013, is appealable per CC 1714.10(d) and Evans v. Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 599, 602-604. The automatic stay provision of CCP 916 applies. While CC 1714.10(d) states that the procedure is a "special proceeding of a civil nature," it also states that an order resolving the matter "shall be appealable as a final judgment in a civil action." Id. This last portion of subsection (d) suggests that the appeal is tantamount to an appeal in a civil action and thus that CCP 916 applies. A stay of the entire action is also reasonable to insure that proceedings in this Court do not create a conflict of interest between attorney Garen and his client defendant PCC, which may affect the appeal and render the appeal ineffective. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CLARK GAREN, ESQ., 310.391.0800, FOR PLAINTIFF/CROSS COMPLAINANT/CROSS DEFENDANT PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANT.
NICOLE SADLER, ESQ., 415.442.1373, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE.
NATHANIEL BIGGER, ESQ., 415.295.4735, FOR DEFENDANT ROBERT LUJAN.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: CROSS DEFENDANT TODD SHIELDS, CLARK GAREN, PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS (PCC), WIRELESS RECEIVABLES ACQUISITION GROUP MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled and the motion to strike is denied. The Court denies PCC's request for judicial notice because it does not cite the precise basis for judicial notice and the attached documents implicate contested factual issues. Mr. Lujan sufficiently alleges the existence of the Cardmember Agreement. The statute of frauds does not apply because there is nothing showing that the contract could not be performed within a year by its terms. Delaware law appears to apply to this action based on paragraph 18 of the contract. PCC fails to show that federal law or tolling applies. Mr. Lujan alleges sufficient facts to hold the individuals and Wireless liable and a violation of B&P 6129. CC 1714.10 does not apply to this case based on the allegations in paragraphs 23-29 in the amended cross complaint. See CC 1714.10(c)(2). 1 ORDER FOR BOTH MOTIONS ON CALENDAR signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR2542.
